name: Vaultwarden
description: Unofficial Bitwarden server written in Rust. Single binary, SQLite by default, drop-in compatible with the official Bitwarden clients. Ideal first install — small footprint, no external database required.
notes: Set an ADMIN_TOKEN env var to enable the /admin panel. Disable SIGNUPS_ALLOWED after creating your first user.
installPromptTemplate:
You are generating a Kubernetes manifest for the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Vaultwarden ({{instance}}) — Bitwarden-compatible password manager.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Persistent storage: {{storage}}Gi
- Extra notes from user: {{notes}}

## Cluster conventions (MUST follow)
- Single Deployment + Service + Ingress + PersistentVolumeClaim, one YAML document each separated by `---`.
- Image: `vaultwarden/server:latest`, `imagePullPolicy: Always`.
- 1 replica (SQLite — no HA).
- Strategy: `Recreate` (PVC is RWO).
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 65534`, `runAsGroup: 65534`, `fsGroup: 65534`. Container: `readOnlyRootFilesystem: false` (Vaultwarden writes to /data). Drop all caps.
- Env: `DOMAIN=https://{{hostname}}`, `SIGNUPS_ALLOWED=true` (user will lock down later), `WEBSOCKET_ENABLED=true`.
- PVC: `{{storage}}Gi`, default storageClass (leave unset).
- Container port 80 (HTTP) and 3012 (WebSocket).
- Service: ClusterIP, expose 80 and 3012.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt-prod`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: default-redirect-https@kubernetescrd`. DO NOT set `router.tls: "true"`. TLS section: `hosts: [{{hostname}}]`, `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Resources: requests cpu 100m / mem 128Mi, limits cpu 500m / mem 512Mi.
- Labels: every resource gets `app.kubernetes.io/name: vaultwarden` and `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- Readiness + liveness probe: `httpGet path=/alive port=80`, initialDelaySeconds 10.
- Image pull secret: `imagePullSecrets: [{name: ghrc}]` (cluster-wide convention even though Docker Hub is public).

## Output
Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml code block containing the full manifest. No prose outside the fence. No comments inside the YAML unless absolutely necessary. The YAML must apply cleanly with `kubectl apply -f -`.

name: Supabase
tagline: Open-source Firebase — Postgres, Auth, Storage, Realtime.
description: Bundled backend: Postgres + GoTrue auth + PostgREST + Realtime + Storage + Kong API gateway + Studio admin UI. Large surface area — multiple Deployments and Services. Expect ~2–4 GiB memory usage at idle.
notes: Multi-service install. Studio admin UI is exposed via the ingress; the Postgres + GoTrue + PostgREST services stay ClusterIP only. Generate JWT secret + anon/service keys before applying.
installPromptTemplate:
You are generating a Kubernetes manifest for the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Supabase ({{instance}}) — open-source Firebase alternative. Self-hosted bundle: Postgres + GoTrue auth + PostgREST + Realtime + Storage + Kong API gateway + Studio.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Public ingress hostname: {{hostname}} (Studio admin UI)
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Postgres PVC size: {{storage}}Gi
- Extra notes from user: {{notes}}

## Cluster conventions (MUST follow)
- Generate placeholders for the secrets the user MUST fill in before applying: `JWT_SECRET` (32+ char random), `ANON_KEY` (JWT signed with JWT_SECRET, role=anon), `SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` (JWT signed with JWT_SECRET, role=service_role), `POSTGRES_PASSWORD`, `DASHBOARD_USERNAME`, `DASHBOARD_PASSWORD`. Surface these as `valueFrom: secretKeyRef` against a single Secret named `{{instance}}-secrets` — and include that Secret as the FIRST document with placeholder values clearly labeled `<FILL_ME_IN>`.
- All Deployments: 1 replica, `Recreate` strategy (PVC is RWO).
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, drop all caps. Postgres uses `runAsUser: 999` (postgres uid in supabase/postgres image).
- Postgres: image `supabase/postgres:15.1.0.147`, PVC `{{storage}}Gi` mounted at `/var/lib/postgresql/data` (subPath `pgdata`).
- GoTrue: image `supabase/gotrue:v2.151.0`, env wired to Postgres via service DNS.
- PostgREST: image `postgrest/postgrest:v12.0.1`.
- Realtime: image `supabase/realtime:v2.25.66`.
- Storage: image `supabase/storage-api:v0.43.11`, PVC `5Gi` for file storage.
- Kong: image `kong:2.8.1`, declarative config via ConfigMap routing `/auth → gotrue:9999`, `/rest → postgrest:3000`, `/storage → storage:5000`, `/realtime → realtime:4000`.
- Studio: image `supabase/studio:20240101-ce42139`.
- Services: all ClusterIP. Only one Ingress, pointing to Studio on port 3000.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt-prod`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: default-redirect-https@kubernetescrd`. DO NOT set `router.tls: "true"`. TLS section: `hosts: [{{hostname}}]`, `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Resources per service: requests 100–300m / 256–512Mi, limits 1 / 1Gi. Postgres gets more: 500m / 1Gi requests, 2 / 4Gi limits.
- Labels: every resource gets `app.kubernetes.io/name: supabase`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`, `app.kubernetes.io/component: <postgres|gotrue|postgrest|realtime|storage|kong|studio>`.
- Image pull secret: `imagePullSecrets: [{name: ghrc}]` on every pod spec.
- Readiness probes on each service appropriate to the image (postgres: `pg_isready`; HTTP services: `httpGet /health` or `/`).

## Output
Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml code block containing the full manifest as multiple `---`-separated documents. No prose outside the fence. The first document MUST be the placeholder `{{instance}}-secrets` Secret with `<FILL_ME_IN>` markers and a top comment instructing the user to fill before applying. The YAML must apply cleanly with `kubectl apply -f -` once secrets are filled.

name: Plausible
description: Privacy-focused Google Analytics alternative. Self-hosted Plausible Analytics dashboard backed by Postgres and ClickHouse. Open source, GDPR-friendly, simple stats UI.
notes: Plausible needs both Postgres (settings + users) AND ClickHouse (events). Bundle the dependencies as StatefulSets with their own PVCs. SECRET_KEY_BASE must be a 64-char base64 string.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Plausible Analytics** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}}
- Postgres PVC: 5Gi; ClickHouse PVC: {{storage}}Gi
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Cluster conventions
- Three workloads: `plausible` (the Phoenix app), `postgres` (StatefulSet), `clickhouse` (StatefulSet).
- Plausible image: `ghcr.io/plausible/community-edition:v2.1.4`.
- Postgres image: `postgres:16-alpine`, ClickHouse image: `clickhouse/clickhouse-server:24.3-alpine`.
- Secret named `{{instance}}-secrets` with `SECRET_KEY_BASE` (64-char base64 placeholder), `TOTP_VAULT_KEY` (32-byte placeholder), Postgres password, ClickHouse password — all `<FILL_ME_IN>`.
- ConfigMap for env: `BASE_URL=https://{{hostname}}`, `DATABASE_URL=postgresql://...`, `CLICKHOUSE_DATABASE_URL=http://...`, `DISABLE_REGISTRATION=invite_only`.
- One replica per workload, `Recreate` strategy, PVCs default storageClass.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, drop all caps. ClickHouse needs `runAsUser: 101`; Postgres `runAsUser: 999`.
- Services: all ClusterIP. Plausible service on port 8000.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt-prod`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: default-redirect-https@kubernetescrd`. DO NOT set `router.tls: "true"`. TLS section: `hosts: [{{hostname}}]`, `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: plausible`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`, `app.kubernetes.io/component: <app|postgres|clickhouse>`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: ghrc}]` on every pod spec.
- Readiness probes: Plausible `GET /api/health`, postgres `pg_isready`, clickhouse `GET /ping`.

## Output
One fenced ```yaml block, multi-document, with the placeholder secret first. No prose outside the fence.

name: Nextcloud
tagline: Self-hosted file sync, share, and collaboration.
description: Personal cloud platform: file sync (Dropbox replacement), shared calendars, contacts, notes. Heavy app — PHP-FPM + nginx + Postgres. Plan on at least 2 GiB RAM and a sizeable PVC.
notes: First-time setup wizard creates the admin user via the web UI — keep SIGNUPS open until then, lock it down after. Database is Postgres in the same namespace; bundled SQLite is not recommended for multi-user use.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Nextcloud** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}}
- Data PVC: {{storage}}Gi; Postgres PVC: 10Gi
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Two workloads: `nextcloud` (Deployment, image `nextcloud:29-apache`) and `postgres` (StatefulSet, image `postgres:16-alpine`).
- Secret `{{instance}}-secrets` with admin password + Postgres password (both `<FILL_ME_IN>`).
- Env on Nextcloud: `POSTGRES_HOST=postgres`, `POSTGRES_DB=nextcloud`, `POSTGRES_USER=nextcloud`, `NEXTCLOUD_TRUSTED_DOMAINS={{hostname}}`, `OVERWRITEPROTOCOL=https`, `OVERWRITECLIURL=https://{{hostname}}`.
- 1 replica, Recreate strategy. Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 33` (www-data).
- Service: ClusterIP, port 80.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt-prod`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: default-redirect-https@kubernetescrd`. TLS section: `hosts: [{{hostname}}]`, `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`. Set `nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-body-size: 0` equivalent for Traefik via Middleware if needed — note this in a comment but don't add unless the user asks.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: nextcloud`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: ghrc}]`.
- Readiness probe: `httpGet /status.php port=80`, initialDelaySeconds 60 (Nextcloud takes a minute to bootstrap on first run).

## Output
One fenced ```yaml block, multi-document, placeholder secret first.

name: Gitea
tagline: Lightweight self-hosted Git service + CI runner.
description: GitHub-flavored Git hosting, issue tracking, and Actions-compatible CI runners — in one binary. Small footprint, Postgres or SQLite backing. Best for personal repos and small teams.
notes: Single-replica only — SQLite by default. For multi-replica HA you'd swap to Postgres + a shared PVC; not covered in v1.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Gitea** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}}
- Repo PVC: {{storage}}Gi
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Single Deployment, image `gitea/gitea:1.22`, 1 replica, Recreate strategy.
- SQLite backing (no separate database pod).
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 1000`, `runAsGroup: 1000`, `fsGroup: 1000`.
- Env: `GITEA__server__DOMAIN={{hostname}}`, `GITEA__server__ROOT_URL=https://{{hostname}}/`, `GITEA__server__SSH_DOMAIN={{hostname}}`, `GITEA__service__DISABLE_REGISTRATION=false` (until first user), `GITEA__database__DB_TYPE=sqlite3`.
- Ports: 3000 (HTTP), 22 (SSH).
- PVC `{{storage}}Gi` mounted at `/data`.
- Service: ClusterIP exposing both 3000 and 22 (SSH won't be reachable from outside the cluster without a NodePort or LoadBalancer; mention in a comment but don't add).
- Ingress: only 3000. `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt-prod`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: default-redirect-https@kubernetescrd`. TLS: `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: gitea`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: ghrc}]`.
- Readiness probe: `httpGet /api/healthz port=3000`, initialDelaySeconds 20.

## Output
One fenced ```yaml block, multi-document. No prose outside the fence.

notes: Page archiving uses headless Chromium — that's where the memory headroom goes. Postgres lives in the same namespace.

- Env on Linkwarden: `DATABASE_URL=postgresql://linkwarden@postgres:5432/linkwarden`, `NEXTAUTH_URL=https://{{hostname}}`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_DISABLE_REGISTRATION=false` (lock down after first user).
- 1 replica per workload, Recreate strategy. Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`.

- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: ghrc}]`.

name: Penpot
description: Free Figma alternative — open-source vector design, prototyping, real-time collaboration. Stack: frontend nginx + backend Clojure + Postgres + Redis. Multi-pod install.
notes: Multi-service: frontend, backend, exporter, postgres, redis. Plan ~1–2 GiB at idle. SMTP needed only if you want email invites.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Penpot** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}}
- Assets PVC: {{storage}}Gi; Postgres PVC: 10Gi; Redis PVC: 1Gi
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Five workloads: `frontend` (nginx, image `penpotapp/frontend:latest`), `backend` (image `penpotapp/backend:latest`), `exporter` (image `penpotapp/exporter:latest`), `postgres` (StatefulSet, `postgres:15-alpine`), `redis` (StatefulSet, `redis:7-alpine`).
- Secret `{{instance}}-secrets`: `PENPOT_SECRET_KEY` (32-byte base64 `<FILL_ME_IN>`), Postgres password.
- Backend env: `PENPOT_PUBLIC_URI=https://{{hostname}}`, `PENPOT_DATABASE_URI=postgresql://postgres:5432/penpot`, `PENPOT_REDIS_URI=redis://redis:6379/0`, `PENPOT_REGISTRATION_ENABLED=true` (lock down after first signup), `PENPOT_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=false`.
- 1 replica per workload, Recreate.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, drop all caps where the image allows.
- Services: all ClusterIP. Only `frontend` ingressed, on port 80.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt-prod`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: default-redirect-https@kubernetescrd`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: penpot`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`, `app.kubernetes.io/component: <frontend|backend|exporter|postgres|redis>`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: ghrc}]` on every pod spec.
- Readiness: frontend `httpGet / port=80`, backend `httpGet /readyz port=6060`, postgres `pg_isready`, redis `redis-cli PING`.

## Output
One fenced ```yaml block, multi-document, placeholder secret first.

name: Sentry
tagline: Open-source error tracking and performance monitoring.
description: Self-hosted Sentry — capture exceptions, traces, profiles, and replays across your apps. Heavy multi-service install: web + worker + cron + ingest consumers + Snuba (events) + ClickHouse + Postgres + Redis + Kafka + Zookeeper + Memcached + Symbolicator. Expect ~8 GiB RAM at idle.
notes: Heavy install — Sentry self-hosted normally ships as docker-compose with 20+ containers. Use the official sentry-kubernetes Helm chart if you want a tighter, supported deployment; this wizard generates a flat manifest only and may need hand-tuning per service.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Sentry** ({{instance}}) self-hosted on the user's k3s cluster.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}}
- Postgres PVC: {{storage}}Gi; ClickHouse PVC: 30Gi; Kafka PVC: 10Gi
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Workloads (one Deployment each unless noted): `sentry-web` (image `getsentry/sentry:25.1.0`, runs `sentry run web`), `sentry-worker` (`sentry run worker`), `sentry-cron` (`sentry run cron`), `sentry-ingest` (`sentry run consumer ingest-events`), `snuba-api` (image `getsentry/snuba:25.1.0`), `snuba-consumer` (`snuba consumer --storage errors`), `relay` (image `getsentry/relay:25.1.0`). Plus dependency StatefulSets: `postgres:15-alpine`, `clickhouse/clickhouse-server:24.3-alpine`, `redis:7-alpine`, `bitnami/kafka:3.5`, `bitnami/zookeeper:3.9`, `memcached:1.6-alpine`.
- Secret `{{instance}}-secrets`: `SENTRY_SECRET_KEY` (50-char `<FILL_ME_IN>`), `POSTGRES_PASSWORD`, `CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD`, initial admin email + password — all `<FILL_ME_IN>`.
- Env on every sentry-* pod: `SENTRY_REDIS_HOST=redis`, `SENTRY_POSTGRES_HOST=postgres`, `SENTRY_KAFKA_HOST=kafka:9092`, `SENTRY_DB_NAME=sentry`, `SENTRY_DB_USER=sentry`, `SENTRY_SECRET_KEY` from secretKeyRef.
- 1 replica per workload (HA is out of scope), `Recreate` strategy.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, drop all caps. Sentry image runs as user 999.
- Services: ClusterIP for everything; only `sentry-web` (port 9000) is ingressed.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt-prod`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: default-redirect-https@kubernetescrd`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Resources: web/worker each 500m/2 cpu, 1Gi/2Gi mem. Snuba and Kafka 500m/2 cpu, 1Gi/3Gi mem. ClickHouse 1/4 cpu, 2Gi/4Gi mem. Postgres 500m/2 cpu, 1Gi/2Gi mem.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: sentry`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`, `app.kubernetes.io/component: <web|worker|cron|ingest|snuba-api|snuba-consumer|relay|postgres|clickhouse|redis|kafka|zookeeper|memcached>`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: ghrc}]` on every pod spec.
- A `sentry-upgrade` Job (Helm-chart-style) that runs `sentry upgrade --noinput` once before web/worker come up — gate it via initContainers or a top-level Job with appropriate ordering.
- Readiness: web `httpGet /_health/ port=9000`, snuba-api `httpGet /health port=1218`, postgres `pg_isready`, clickhouse `httpGet /ping port=8123`, redis `redis-cli PING`.

## Output
One fenced ```yaml block, multi-document, placeholder secret first. Add a top-level comment block above the secret explaining the upgrade Job + post-install user creation step.

name: SigNoz
tagline: Open-source DataDog — APM, metrics, logs, traces.
description: Full-stack OpenTelemetry-based observability platform. Components: query-service, frontend, otel-collector (ingestion), ClickHouse (storage), Zookeeper, alertmanager. SigNoz is the obvious choice when you want one tool for APM + metrics + logs.
notes: ClickHouse storage grows quickly under real traffic. Repo already has signoz-values.yaml + signoz-tailscale-svc.yaml from a Helm install — favor those for production; this wizard generates a flat YAML for a fresh standalone install.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **SigNoz** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}}
- ClickHouse PVC: {{storage}}Gi; SigNoz query-service PVC: 5Gi
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Workloads: `query-service` (Deployment, image `signoz/query-service:0.51.0`), `frontend` (Deployment, `signoz/frontend:0.51.0`), `otel-collector` (Deployment, `signoz/signoz-otel-collector:0.111.0`), `alertmanager` (Deployment, `signoz/alertmanager:0.23.7`), `clickhouse` (StatefulSet, `clickhouse/clickhouse-server:24.1.2-alpine`), `zookeeper` (StatefulSet, `zookeeper:3.7.1`).
- ConfigMaps for: clickhouse server config (storage_configuration + macros), otel-collector pipelines (receivers, processors, exporters → clickhousedb).
- Secret `{{instance}}-secrets`: `CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD` `<FILL_ME_IN>`.
- Env on query-service: `ClickHouseUrl=tcp://clickhouse:9000`, `STORAGE=clickhouse`, `ALERTMANAGER_API_PREFIX=http://alertmanager:9093/api`, `SIGNOZ_LOCAL_DB_PATH=/var/lib/signoz/signoz.db`.
- 1 replica per workload, Recreate strategy.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, drop all caps where the image allows. ClickHouse runs as user 101.
- Services: all ClusterIP. Only `frontend` (port 3301) ingressed externally; the OTLP collector ports (4317 grpc, 4318 http) stay internal — note in a comment that the user can expose them via a Tailscale Service if remote ingestion is desired.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt-prod`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: default-redirect-https@kubernetescrd`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Resources: query-service 300m/1500m, 512Mi/1Gi. frontend 50m/200m, 64Mi/256Mi. otel-collector 500m/2 cpu, 512Mi/2Gi. ClickHouse 1/4 cpu, 2Gi/4Gi.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: signoz`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`, `app.kubernetes.io/component: <query-service|frontend|otel-collector|alertmanager|clickhouse|zookeeper>`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: ghrc}]`.
- Readiness: query-service `httpGet /api/v1/health port=8080`, frontend `httpGet / port=3301`, clickhouse `httpGet /ping port=8123`, otel-collector `httpGet / port=13133`.

name: PostHog
tagline: Open-source product analytics, feature flags, session replays.
description: All-in-one product analytics: event capture, funnels, retention, A/B tests, feature flags, session replays. ClickHouse for analytics, Postgres for app state, Kafka for ingestion, Redis for caching.
notes: PostHog official self-hosted is Helm. The flat-YAML route here will need the user to fill in JWT/secret keys and may lag behind upstream feature work.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}}
- ClickHouse PVC: {{storage}}Gi; Postgres PVC: 10Gi; Kafka PVC: 10Gi
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Workloads: `posthog-web` (Deployment, image `posthog/posthog:1.50.0`, command runs `./bin/docker-server`), `posthog-worker` (`./bin/docker-worker`), `plugin-server` (Deployment, `posthog/posthog:1.50.0`, runs `./bin/plugin-server`), `postgres` (StatefulSet, `postgres:15-alpine`), `redis` (StatefulSet, `redis:7-alpine`), `clickhouse` (StatefulSet, `clickhouse/clickhouse-server:24.1-alpine`), `kafka` (StatefulSet, `bitnami/kafka:3.5`), `zookeeper` (StatefulSet, `bitnami/zookeeper:3.9`).
- Secret `{{instance}}-secrets`: `SECRET_KEY` (50-char Django key `<FILL_ME_IN>`), `POSTGRES_PASSWORD`, `CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD`, `KAFKA_PASSWORD` (`<FILL_ME_IN>`).
- Env on web/worker/plugin-server: `DATABASE_URL=postgres://posthog@postgres:5432/posthog`, `REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379/0`, `CLICKHOUSE_HOST=clickhouse`, `CLICKHOUSE_DATABASE=posthog`, `KAFKA_HOSTS=kafka:9092`, `SITE_URL=https://{{hostname}}`, `DISABLE_SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT=False`.
- 1 replica per workload, Recreate.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, drop all caps. Postgres `runAsUser: 999`; ClickHouse `runAsUser: 101`.
- Services: all ClusterIP. Only `posthog-web` (port 8000) ingressed.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt-prod`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: default-redirect-https@kubernetescrd`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Resources: web/worker each 300m/1500m, 512Mi/1.5Gi. plugin-server 500m/2 cpu, 1Gi/2Gi. ClickHouse 1/4 cpu, 2Gi/4Gi. Postgres 500m/2 cpu, 1Gi/2Gi.
- An init Job that runs `python manage.py migrate` before web/worker come up.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: posthog`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`, `app.kubernetes.io/component: <web|worker|plugin-server|postgres|redis|clickhouse|kafka|zookeeper>`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: ghrc}]`.
- Readiness: web `httpGet /_health/ port=8000`, postgres `pg_isready`, clickhouse `httpGet /ping port=8123`, redis `redis-cli PING`, kafka `tcpSocket port=9092`.

name: Excalidraw
tagline: Virtual whiteboard for sketchy hand-drawn diagrams.
description: Frontend-only collaborative whiteboard. Single static-served container, no backend state. Loads instantly, drawings persist in browser localStorage by default. Already deployed in this cluster — this entry is a clean template for a second instance.
notes: Stateless. For multi-user real-time collaboration you'd also deploy excalidraw-room (the WebSocket sync server) — not included here; the default UI works for single-user persistent-in-browser use.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Excalidraw** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}}
- No persistent storage (stateless).
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Single Deployment, image `excalidraw/excalidraw:latest`, `imagePullPolicy: Always`.
- 2 replicas, `RollingUpdate` (maxSurge 1, maxUnavailable 0) — it's stateless.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 101`, `runAsGroup: 101`, `fsGroup: 101` (nginx user inside the image). Container: `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true`, drop all caps.
- emptyDir mounts at `/var/cache/nginx`, `/var/run`, `/tmp` so the read-only-root nginx can still write its runtime files.
- Container port 8080.
- Service: ClusterIP, port 80 → targetPort 8080.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt-prod`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: default-redirect-https@kubernetescrd`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Resources: requests 25m / 32Mi, limits 200m / 128Mi.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: excalidraw`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: ghrc}]`.
- Readiness + liveness probe: `httpGet / port=8080`, initialDelaySeconds 3.

name: Twenty

notes: Single-tenant install. Workspace is created on first login through the web UI.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Twenty CRM** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}}
- Postgres PVC: {{storage}}Gi; Server data PVC: 5Gi
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Workloads: `twenty-server` (Deployment, image `twentycrm/twenty:latest`, runs server + GraphQL on 3000), `twenty-worker` (Deployment, same image, runs `yarn worker:prod`), `postgres` (StatefulSet, `postgres:16-alpine`), `redis` (StatefulSet, `redis:7-alpine`).
- Secret `{{instance}}-secrets`: `APP_SECRET` (32-byte base64 `<FILL_ME_IN>`), `ACCESS_TOKEN_SECRET`, `REFRESH_TOKEN_SECRET`, `LOGIN_TOKEN_SECRET`, `FILE_TOKEN_SECRET` (each 32-byte base64), `POSTGRES_PASSWORD`.
- Env on server + worker: `PG_DATABASE_URL=postgres://twenty@postgres:5432/twenty`, `REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379`, `SERVER_URL=https://{{hostname}}`, `FRONT_BASE_URL=https://{{hostname}}`, `IS_SIGN_UP_DISABLED=false` (lock down after first user), `STORAGE_TYPE=local`, `STORAGE_LOCAL_PATH=/app/.local-storage`.
- 1 replica per workload, Recreate.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 1000`, `fsGroup: 1000` on twenty pods.
- Server data PVC mounted at `/app/.local-storage`.
- Services: all ClusterIP. `twenty-server` exposes port 3000.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt-prod`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: default-redirect-https@kubernetescrd`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Resources: server 300m/1500m, 512Mi/1.5Gi. worker 200m/1000m, 256Mi/1Gi.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: twenty`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`, `app.kubernetes.io/component: <server|worker|postgres|redis>`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: ghrc}]`.
- An init Job that runs `yarn database:reset` (or `yarn database:migrate:prod`) before the server pod starts — keep it idempotent.
- Readiness: server `httpGet /healthz port=3000` initialDelaySeconds 20.

name: Postiz
description: Schedule, cross-post, and queue content across Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Bluesky, Mastodon, Threads, Discord, and more. Stack: app + worker + cron + Postgres + Redis. Already deployed in this cluster — this entry is a clean template for a second instance.
notes: Per-platform OAuth credentials are configured via env vars in the secret. Leave the social-platform secret fields blank and add only the ones you want to integrate.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Postiz** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}}
- Postgres PVC: {{storage}}Gi; Uploads PVC: 5Gi
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Workloads: `postiz-app` (Deployment, image `ghcr.io/gitroomhq/postiz-app:latest`, runs the web + API), `postiz-worker` (Deployment, same image, runs `pnpm run start:prod:workers`), `postiz-cron` (Deployment, same image, runs `pnpm run start:prod:cron`), `postgres` (StatefulSet, `postgres:16-alpine`), `redis` (StatefulSet, `redis:7-alpine`).
- Secret `{{instance}}-secrets`: `DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postiz@postgres:5432/postiz`, `REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379`, `JWT_SECRET` (32-byte `<FILL_ME_IN>`), `POSTGRES_PASSWORD`, plus optional `X_API_KEY`, `LINKEDIN_CLIENT_ID`, `LINKEDIN_CLIENT_SECRET`, etc. — leave blank when not using the corresponding platform.
- Env on every postiz-* pod: pull `DATABASE_URL`, `REDIS_URL`, `JWT_SECRET` from the secret. Plus `FRONTEND_URL=https://{{hostname}}`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL=https://{{hostname}}/api`, `BACKEND_INTERNAL_URL=http://postiz-app:3000`, `STORAGE_PROVIDER=local`, `UPLOAD_DIRECTORY=/uploads`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_UPLOAD_DIRECTORY=/uploads`, `MAIN_URL=https://{{hostname}}`.
- 1 replica per workload, Recreate.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 1000`, `fsGroup: 1000` for postiz pods.
- Uploads PVC mounted at `/uploads` on app + worker.
- Services: all ClusterIP. `postiz-app` exposes ports 3000 (frontend) and 3001 (backend) — Postiz combines both behind a single nginx layer in the image; ingress points at port 4200 (the bundled router) or whichever the image exposes by default; verify against the upstream Dockerfile.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt-prod`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: default-redirect-https@kubernetescrd`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Resources: app 300m/1500m, 512Mi/1.5Gi. worker + cron each 200m/1000m, 256Mi/1Gi.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: postiz`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`, `app.kubernetes.io/component: <app|worker|cron|postgres|redis>`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: ghrc}]`.
- Init Job: `pnpm run prisma-db-push` to migrate the schema before app starts.
- Readiness: app `httpGet / port=4200` initialDelaySeconds 30 (the app boots slowly).

description: Node-RED-style visual workflow builder with 400+ integrations. SQLite by default (fine for single-replica); switch to Postgres for HA. Webhook receiver + cron triggers + execution worker all in one container.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}}

## Conventions
- Single Deployment, image `n8nio/n8n:latest`, 1 replica, Recreate.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 1000`, `runAsGroup: 1000`, `fsGroup: 1000` (image's `node` user).
- PVC `{{storage}}Gi` mounted at `/home/node/.n8n`.
- Env: `N8N_HOST={{hostname}}`, `WEBHOOK_URL=https://{{hostname}}/`, `N8N_PROTOCOL=https`, `N8N_PORT=5678`, `GENERIC_TIMEZONE=UTC`, `N8N_RUNNERS_ENABLED=true` (use the new task-runner subsystem), `DB_TYPE=sqlite`.
- Secret `{{instance}}-secrets`: `N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY` (32-byte base64 `<FILL_ME_IN>`) — required so credentials persist across pod restarts.
- Container port 5678.
- Service: ClusterIP, port 80 → targetPort 5678.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt-prod`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: default-redirect-https@kubernetescrd`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Resources: requests 200m / 256Mi, limits 1 / 1Gi.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: n8n`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: ghrc}]`.
- Readiness probe: `httpGet /healthz port=5678` initialDelaySeconds 10.
